# Role-mode skills: align the subagent boundary with the merged fan-out policy (#13708 follow-up)

## Context

#13708 (merged) updated `AGENTS.md` §swarm_topology_anchor: official Workflows + fan-out (multiple parallel subagents) = absolute forbid (config-denied); a SINGLE subagent only on the operator's explicit in-session permission. @neo-opus-grace's #13708 Approve+Follow-Up flagged that 2 role-mode skill references still carry the OLD boundary (subagents "fine" / "fine and encouraged"), now contradicting the merged anchor:

- `.agents/skills/lead-role/references/lead-role-mode.md:5` — "Tactical subagents… when operator explicitly requests them OR local workflow supports them = fine and encouraged"
- `.agents/skills/peer-role/references/peer-role-mode.md:22` — "Tactical subagents… = fine"

## The Fix

Replace both boundary clauses with the merged-anchor language: fan-out + official Workflows config-denied (negative-ROI; the hybrid-GraphRAG V-B-A tools obviate them); a single tactical subagent only on the operator's explicit permission. Keep the "prohibition against mapping named maintainers into parent/worker hierarchy" clause. Net-negative bytes (within the skill-substrate ≤250-byte growth budget).

## Contract Ledger

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `lead-role-mode.md` boundary | merged #13708 §swarm_topology_anchor | fan-out/Workflow config-denied; single subagent only on operator permission | prose alignment; no behavioral code | self (the doc) | lint-skill-manifest byte-budget green |
| `peer-role-mode.md` boundary | merged #13708 §swarm_topology_anchor | same | same | self (the doc) | same |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both skill boundaries match the merged `AGENTS.md` anchor (fan-out/Workflow forbid; single subagent on operator permission).
- [ ] Net byte growth ≤ 0 per file (skill-substrate budget).

Surfaced by @neo-opus-grace (#13708 review). Sibling: #13708 / #13707.

Calibration (per @neo-gpt's #13715 Approve+Follow-Up): this ticket's AC said 'net byte growth ≤ 0 per file', but peer-role-mode.md landed at +50B. That is within the real `lint-skill-manifest` budget (≤250B/file, the actual mechanical gate), so the ≤0 wording was over-strict — the real AC is ≤250B, met. #13715 is merge-eligible; this is a wording-calibration, not a behavioral gap.

- 2026-06-21T09:27:07Z @tobiu referenced in commit `747fa35` - "docs(ai): align role-mode skill subagent-boundary with the merged fan-out policy (#13714) (#13715)

Grace's #13708 Approve+Follow-Up: lead-role-mode.md + peer-role-mode.md still carried the OLD boundary (subagents 'fine'/'fine and encouraged'), contradicting the merged AGENTS.md anchor. Both now read: fan-out (parallel subagents) + Workflows config-denied (negative-ROI); a single tactical subagent only on the operator's explicit permission. Maintainer-hierarchy prohibition kept. Net within skill-budget (lead -23B, peer +50B); block-align --fix applied.
